info.title: Location and Timezone API
info.description:
City search and geocoding API covering 7,000+ cities across 227 countries. Returns latitude, longitude, IANA timezone, and DST-aware UTC offset. Built as the location layer for birth chart forms, horoscope apps, event planners, and any feature that needs place-to-coordinates resolution.

# RoxyAPI: AI-Native Insight Infrastructure

> **Base URL:** `https://roxyapi.com/api/v2`
> All endpoint paths below are relative to this base URL.

The only multi-domain spiritual intelligence API. 9 domains (astrology, tarot, numerology, crystals, dreams, I-Ching, angel numbers, Vedic astrology, location), 122+ endpoints, one API key, instant activation. Native MCP support for AI agents.

## Who uses RoxyAPI

- **Developers** building astrology apps, tarot platforms, numerology calculators, or dream journals
- **AI agent builders** connecting Claude, GPT, or Gemini to real calculation engines via MCP
- **Vibe coders** shipping insight apps with Cursor, Bolt, or Replit using zero domain knowledge
- **Founders and brands** launching branded spiritual experiences for their audience

## Quick start (60 seconds)

**1. Get your API key** at [roxyapi.com/pricing](https://roxyapi.com/pricing). Instant delivery, no account required.

**2. Make your first call:**
```bash
curl -H "X-API-Key: YOUR_KEY" https://roxyapi.com/api/v2/tarot/draw -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"count": 3}'
```

**3. Monitor usage:**
```bash
curl -H "X-API-Key: YOUR_KEY" https://roxyapi.com/api/v2/usage
```

## AI agent integration (MCP)

RoxyAPI has native Model Context Protocol support. Your AI agent auto-discovers all 122+ endpoints as callable tools with zero configuration:
- **Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf**: Add MCP server URL in settings
- **OpenAI Agents, Gemini ADK**: Connect via Streamable HTTP transport
- **Custom agents**: Use the MCP Python/TypeScript SDK

MCP endpoints: `https://roxyapi.com/mcp/{domain}` (e.g., `/mcp/astrology-api`, `/mcp/tarot-api`)

Setup guide: [roxyapi.com/docs/mcp](https://roxyapi.com/docs/mcp)

## Authentication

All endpoints require an API key via header or query param:
- **Header (recommended):** `X-API-Key: YOUR_KEY`
- **Query param (testing):** `?api_key=YOUR_KEY`

## Response format

Clean JSON, no wrapper objects. Errors return `{ "error": "message", "code": "error_code" }`. The `error` field is human-readable (may change wording). The `code` field is machine-readable (stable — safe to switch on programmatically).

Rate limit headers on every response: `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Used`

## Errors

All errors return `{ "error": "message", "code": "error_code" }`:

| Status | Code | When |
|--------|------|------|
| 400 | `validation_error` | Missing or invalid parameters |
| 401 | `api_key_required` | No API key provided |
| 401 | `invalid_api_key` | Key format invalid or tampered |
| 401 | `subscription_not_found` | Key references non-existent subscription |
| 401 | `subscription_inactive` | Subscription cancelled, expired, or suspended |
| 404 | `not_found` | Resource not found |
| 429 | `rate_limit_exceeded` | Monthly quota reached |
| 500 | `internal_error` | Server error |

## Pricing

Flat per-request pricing. Every call counts the same, whether a planet position or a full birth chart with aspects. No credit systems, no variable costs. Plans from $39 to $699/month.

See [roxyapi.com/pricing](https://roxyapi.com/pricing)

## Resources

- [Quickstart guide](https://roxyapi.com/docs/quickstart) - first API call in 60 seconds
- [Documentation](https://roxyapi.com/docs) - guides, tutorials, domain reference
- [MCP setup](https://roxyapi.com/docs/mcp) - connect AI agents
- [Starter apps](https://roxyapi.com/starters) - clone and deploy in 30 minutes
- [FAQ](https://roxyapi.com/faq) - common questions
- [Contact](https://roxyapi.com/contact) - support and API key recovery

tags.name: Location and Timezone
tags.description: City search and geocoding API covering 7,000+ cities across 227 countries. Returns latitude, longitude, IANA timezone, and DST-aware UTC offset. Built as the location layer for birth chart forms, horoscope apps, event planners, and any feature that needs place-to-coordinates resolution.

apiKey.name: X-API-Key
apiKey.description: Your API key for accessing RoxyAPI. Alternatively, you can pass the API key as a query parameter "api_key".

get.summary: Search cities worldwide - Geocoding autocomplete with coordinates and timezone
get.description: City autocomplete and geocoding search across 7,000+ locations in 227 countries. Returns geographic coordinates (latitude, longitude), IANA timezone, and DST-aware UTC offset for each match. Built for birth chart location pickers, horoscope apps, event scheduling, and any feature requiring place-to-coordinates resolution. Partial name matching with intelligent ranking: exact prefix matches first, then sorted by population for relevance. Covers all major cities in Europe, Americas, Asia, Africa, and Oceania.

schema.description: City name to search for. Matches against city name, province/state, or combined "city country" queries. Case-insensitive with partial matching (e.g. "ber" matches Berlin, Bern, Bergen).

parameters.description: City name to search for. Matches against city name, province/state, or combined "city country" queries. Case-insensitive with partial matching (e.g. "ber" matches Berlin, Bern, Bergen).
parameters.name: q

schema.description: Maximum items to return per page. Range: 1-50, default 10.

parameters.description: Maximum items to return per page. Range: 1-50, default 10.
parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Number of items to skip for pagination. Default 0.

parameters.description: Number of items to skip for pagination. Default 0.
parameters.name: offset

200.description: Matching cities sorted by relevance (prefix match first) then population

total.description: Total number of cities matching the search query.

limit.description: Page size used for this response.

offset.description: Number of cities skipped. Use with limit for pagination.

city.description: City name as commonly used. Matches the local or internationally recognized name for the location.

province.description: State, province, canton, or administrative region. Helps disambiguate cities with the same name across regions (e.g. Springfield IL vs Springfield MO).

iso2.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Use for filtering cities by country or building country-specific location pickers.

latitude.description: Geographic latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). Pass directly to birth chart, natal chart, horoscope, synastry, transit, kundli, and panchang API endpoints as the latitude parameter.

longitude.description: Geographic longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Pass directly to astrology, horoscope, and panchang API endpoints alongside latitude.

timezone.description: IANA timezone identifier following the tz database standard (e.g. Europe/Berlin, America/New_York, Asia/Tokyo). Use with JavaScript Date, Luxon, day.js, or any date library for accurate local time conversion.

utcOffset.description: Current UTC offset in decimal hours, automatically adjusted for daylight saving time. Pass directly as the timezone parameter in astrology API endpoints. Examples: 1 for CET, 2 for CEST, -5 for EST, 5.5 for IST, 5.75 for Nepal.

population.description: City population estimate from geographic databases. Larger cities rank higher in search results, ensuring major metropolitan areas appear first in autocomplete suggestions.

items.description: Geographic location with coordinates, timezone, and UTC offset. Every field is designed for direct use as input parameters in astrology, horoscope, and location-dependent API calculations.

cities.description: City results for the current page, sorted by relevance (prefix match first) then population.

error.description: Human-readable error message. May change wording.
Human-readable error message. May change wording.
Human-readable error message. May change wording.
Human-readable error message. May change wording.

get.summary: List all 227 countries - ISO codes and city coverage
get.description: Returns all 227 countries with ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 and alpha-3 codes, plus the number of searchable cities per country. Use this endpoint to build country dropdown menus, regional filters, or to check city coverage before querying. Sorted alphabetically by country name. Covers Europe, Americas, Asia, Middle East, Africa, and Oceania.

schema.description: Maximum items to return per page. Range: 1-50, default 50.

parameters.description: Maximum items to return per page. Range: 1-50, default 50.
parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Number of items to skip for pagination. Default 0.

parameters.description: Number of items to skip for pagination. Default 0.
parameters.name: offset

200.description: Alphabetically sorted list of all 227 countries with ISO codes and city counts

limit.description: Page size used for this response.

offset.description: Number of countries skipped. Use with limit for pagination.

name.description: Full country name in English. Use for display in location pickers and dropdown menus.

iso2.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Use as the identifier when fetching cities for a specific country via the /countries/{iso2} endpoint.

iso3.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code. Three-letter standard used in international data exchange.

cityCount.description: Number of searchable cities available for this country. Useful for showing coverage in UI or deciding whether to offer city search for a given country.

items.description: Country with ISO 3166 codes and city coverage count. Use iso2 to query cities within a specific country.

countries.description: Countries for the current page, sorted alphabetically by name.

error.description: Human-readable error message. May change wording.
Human-readable error message. May change wording.
Human-readable error message. May change wording.
Human-readable error message. May change wording.

get.summary: Get cities in a country - Geocoding directory sorted by population
get.description: Returns all cities for a specific country, identified by ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code (e.g. DE for Germany, FR for France, GB for United Kingdom, US for United States). Each city includes geographic coordinates, IANA timezone, and DST-aware UTC offset for direct use in astrology birth chart, horoscope, transit, and panchang calculations. Cities sorted by population with the largest metropolitan areas first.

schema.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, case-insensitive. Common codes: DE (Germany), FR (France), GB (United Kingdom), US (United States), ES (Spain), IT (Italy), NL (Netherlands), IN (India), BR (Brazil), JP (Japan).

parameters.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, case-insensitive. Common codes: DE (Germany), FR (France), GB (United Kingdom), US (United States), ES (Spain), IT (Italy), NL (Netherlands), IN (India), BR (Brazil), JP (Japan).
parameters.name: iso2

schema.description: Maximum items to return per page. Range: 1-200, default 50.

parameters.description: Maximum items to return per page. Range: 1-200, default 50.
parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Number of items to skip for pagination. Default 0.

parameters.description: Number of items to skip for pagination. Default 0.
parameters.name: offset

200.description: Cities in the specified country, sorted by population (largest first)

total.description: Total number of cities available for this country.

limit.description: Page size used for this response.

offset.description: Number of cities skipped. Use with limit for pagination.

city.description: City name as commonly used. Matches the local or internationally recognized name for the location.

province.description: State, province, canton, or administrative region. Helps disambiguate cities with the same name across regions (e.g. Springfield IL vs Springfield MO).

iso2.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Use for filtering cities by country or building country-specific location pickers.

latitude.description: Geographic latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). Pass directly to birth chart, natal chart, horoscope, synastry, transit, kundli, and panchang API endpoints as the latitude parameter.

longitude.description: Geographic longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Pass directly to astrology, horoscope, and panchang API endpoints alongside latitude.

timezone.description: IANA timezone identifier following the tz database standard (e.g. Europe/Berlin, America/New_York, Asia/Tokyo). Use with JavaScript Date, Luxon, day.js, or any date library for accurate local time conversion.

utcOffset.description: Current UTC offset in decimal hours, automatically adjusted for daylight saving time. Pass directly as the timezone parameter in astrology API endpoints. Examples: 1 for CET, 2 for CEST, -5 for EST, 5.5 for IST, 5.75 for Nepal.

population.description: City population estimate from geographic databases. Larger cities rank higher in search results, ensuring major metropolitan areas appear first in autocomplete suggestions.

items.description: Geographic location with coordinates, timezone, and UTC offset. Every field is designed for direct use as input parameters in astrology, horoscope, and location-dependent API calculations.

cities.description: Cities for the current page, sorted by population (largest first).
